claims.statement: Warp metric-family definition anchors are only admissible at diagnostic tier when they remain explicitly subordinate to the shared Einstein-equation baseline and stress-energy source semantics, rather than being treated as standalone viability claims.

constraints: definition anchors remain below certified viability posture
equation binding must remain the shared Einstein-equation baseline
source-backed family anchors do not imply runtime admissibility without downstream guardrail and residual evidence

sources.note: Canonical zero-expansion and ADM-shift family source.

sources.citation: Van Den Broeck (1999), Class. Quantum Grav. 16(12), DOI 10.1088/0264-9381/16/12/314.

sources.note: Shared GR metric / field-equation / stress-energy / units / viability foundation lane.

claims.notes: This claim intentionally stops at definition-anchor maturity. It does not certify a warp solution or physical admissibility.

claims.statement: The warp geometry stack is only admissible as a diagnostic GR bridge when its metric-family anchors are explicitly linked to the shared metric, Einstein-equation, stress-energy, units, and viability foundations used elsewhere in the repo.

constraints: bridge node must bind to the Einstein-equation baseline
bridge remains definitional until downstream residual and guardrail lanes are attached
proxy-only lanes such as Ford-Roman remain distinct from theorem-equivalent geometry anchors

sources.note: Warp geometry definition anchors and bridge node.
Shared GR baseline and stress-energy bridge.

sources.equation: G_mu_nu + Lambda * g_mu_nu = (8 * pi * G / c^4) * T_mu_nu
sources.note: Canonical equation binding used by the bridge.

claims.notes: This claim exists so warp-tree bridge nodes satisfy the repo's claim/equation rails without pretending that definition-level geometry anchors are already runtime-certified.
